source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
The diagram shows a square and an equilateral right-angled crossshaped dodecagon. The length of the perimeter of the dodecagon is $36 \mathrm{~cm}$. What, in $\mathrm{cm}^{2}$, is the area of the square?
<image1>

issues: metadata.image_paths does not match top-level image_paths

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says 'The diagram shows' and asks about a square and a cross-shaped dodecagon
the geometric relationship needed to connect the dodecagon perimeter to the square area is not fully specified in text alone
multi_step: solving appears to require using the dodecagon perimeter 36 cm to infer side length information
the task then requires relating the dodecagon dimensions to the square's dimensions from the diagram
final output is an area, implying at least one additional transformation after extracting linear measurements
verifiability: answer_text is a single concrete value: 72
this is a geometry problem with a determinate target quantity, making rule-based or symbolic checking plausible
